test_description: For each map, form a large-scale temperature field by retaining multipoles 2<=ell<=64, then evaluate only the unmasked pixels and subtract their median. Let q05, q50, and q95 be the 5th, 50th, and 95th percentiles of these unmasked pixel temperatures. The single anomaly statistic is S = ln[(q95 - q50)/(q50 - q05)]. Negative values indicate an unusually extended cold tail, positive values an unusually extended hot tail. Compute the same scalar for the Planck SMICA map and for each of the 1000 LCDM simulations using the identical filtering and mask.
test_hypothesis: The Planck masked low-ℓ temperature field has an anomalous hot/cold tail asymmetry relative to ΛCDM simulations, meaning its log-ratio statistic S is unusually far from the simulation expectation in either direction.
justificaton: The statistic is signed, with negative S indicating an extended cold tail and positive S indicating an extended hot tail, but the test description and code do not specify an a priori preference for either direction. Since the anomaly could be either a cold-tail or hot-tail excess, the appropriate default is a two-tailed test.

custom_summary.test_description: Masked Low-l Tail-Asymmetry Log-Ratio: retain multipoles 2<=ell<=64, evaluate only originally unmasked pixels, subtract the median, and return S = ln[(q95-q50)/(q50-q05)]. Negative S indicates an extended cold tail; positive S indicates an extended hot tail.
custom_summary.statistic: S = ln[(q95 - q50)/(q50 - q05)] on originally unmasked pixels of the 2<=ell<=64 filtered temperature field after median subtraction.
custom_summary.mask_handling: Pixels with non-finite input values are excluded from the percentile calculation. They are filled with 0.0 only for the healpy spherical-harmonic transform.
custom_summary.sign_convention: S < 0 means a wider cold tail; S > 0 means a wider hot tail.

plot_spec.title: Masked low-l tail-asymmetry log-ratio
plot_spec.caption: Simulation distribution of the signed low-l tail-asymmetry statistic; the Planck value is compared to this distribution.

custom_summary.interpretation: Planck is not in the most extreme 5% of simulations for this low-l tail-asymmetry statistic.

test_summary.Description: Low-ℓ temperature maps filtered to 2≤ℓ≤64 were evaluated on unmasked pixels after median subtraction using S = ln[(q95−q50)/(q50−q05)], where negative S indicates a relatively extended cold tail and positive S an extended hot tail.
test_summary.Results: Planck SMICA gives S = -0.05498, on the cold-tail side. The 1000 ΛCDM simulations have mean S = 0.00043 and standard deviation 0.05128. Planck is about -1.08σ from the simulation mean, at the 14.3rd percentile, with a two-tailed p-value of 0.286.
test_summary.Interpretation: The Planck value is mildly negative but well within the ΛCDM simulation distribution. It is not close to the 5% two-tailed anomaly threshold, so this test provides no evidence for anomalous low-ℓ hot/cold tail asymmetry.
test_summary.Comparison to literature: Related one-point/non-Gaussianity tests, including skewness, kurtosis, peak statistics, N-point functions and Minkowski functionals, are discussed in Planck Collaboration 2015, A&A 594, A16 and Planck Collaboration 2020, A&A 641, A7. Those works generally found one-point statistics consistent with Gaussian ΛCDM, while confirming other large-scale anomalies such as low variance, the Cold Spot and hemispherical asymmetry. This result is consistent with the non-detection in those related distributional tests.
